claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 13 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my daughter Alex. She's 13 years old. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information! I can see you have a daughter, Alex, who is 13 years old.

To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you some questions:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she does. Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

**Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 per year?** 

(This won't affect your eligibility to receive Child Benefit, but it may mean you'd need to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us earns over £60,000 a year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 13 years old (under 16), which meets the age requirement
- She lives with you in the UK, which means you're responsible for her
- You live in the UK, satisfying the residence requirement  
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her

As an added benefit, since neither you nor your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you won't need to worry about the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit for Alex. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old, which is under 16, so she meets the age requirement. She lives with you in the UK, which means you are responsible for her care. You live in the UK, which satisfies the residence requirement. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which means there is no conflict of claims. Based on these factors, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for your daughter Alex. She is 13 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and no one else is claiming for her. You meet all the standard eligibility requirements. Additionally, since neither you nor your partner earns over £60,000, you won't need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 13 years old, which is under 16, so she meets the age requirement. She lives with you in the UK, which means you are responsible for her care. You live in the UK, which satisfies the residence requirement. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which means there is no conflict of claims. Based on these factors, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: You are eligible for Child Benefit for your daughter Alex. She is 13 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and no one else is claiming for her. You meet all the standard eligibility requirements. Additionally, since neither you nor your partner earns over £60,000, you won't need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.
